titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Poetry Box archive
titlestmt.author: compiled by Meg Rinn, Emily Crow, Yale Center for British Art staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Yale Center for British Art, Archives

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Yale Center for British Art, Archives

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Tuesday February 3, 2026 at 18:00

corpname.part: Yale Center for British Art, Archives

did.physdesc: (3 document boxes + 2 oversize boxes)
did.abstract: This collection documents the creation and exhibition of Eileen Hogan's work The Poetry Box, which serves as an English language version of the Japanese game uta-garuta (歌ガルタ). It includes the art for both the front and back of all eighty cards that comprise the set, documentation regarding the creation of the Poetry Box, and material related to its exhibition.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Poetry Box archive is the physical property of the Yale Center for British Art. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the Archives Department.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This collection is arranged into three series: Series I. Production, Series II. Paintings, and Series III. Exhibition

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Poetry Box archive, Yale Center for British Art Archives.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This collection documents the creation and exhibition of Eileen Hogan's work The Poetry Box, which serves as an English language version of the Japanese game uta-garuta (歌ガルタ). It includes the art for both the front and back of all eighty cards that comprise the set, along with unused works, and proofs for the cards and booklets published with the final set.

Also present is material related to the production and exhibition of the Poetry Box, such as correspondence with selectors, interviews with selectors recorded on cassette tapes, grant related material, and the original prototype of the Poetry Box itself. The Yale Center for British Art Rare Books and Manuscripts department holds one of the final copies of the work. Exhibition documentation is also present, including the original "Acts of renewal" catalog that inspired the work, and items relating to the Poetry Box's display at the San Fransico Center for the Book in 2007.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Box 1: Restricted material. For further information, please contact the Yale Center for British Art Archive.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Eileen Hogan is a landscape and figurative painter based in London.

Born in London in March,1946, Hogan drew extensively as a child. She originally took drawing classes at the Camberwell School of Arts and Crafts (now Camberwell College of Arts) as a fourteen year old and became a full time student in 1962, completing her degree in 1967. She focused on painting with a subsidiary in lettering and graduated with honors.

Hogan continued her education at the Royal Academy Schools (1967-1970), the British School at Athens (1970-1971), and Royal College of Art (1971-1974). She exhibited extensively, beginning in the 1970s, teaching fine art at Camberwell and the University of the Arts London, and giving lectures in Europe and America. She founded the Camberwell Press in 1984 and was named Dean of the School of Applied and Graphic Arts at Camberwell.

Her first solo exhibition took place in 1980 at the Fine Art Society in London. Major commissions include illustrating On common Ground: A Programme for teaching Poetry in 1987, stamps for the Royal Mail (1990, 1991, 1993, 2001), portraits of World War II veterans at the behest of His Royal Highness The Prince of Wales in 2015-2016, and portraits of both His Royal Highness the Prince of Wales and Her Royal Highness the Duchess of Cornwall in 2016 to 2017. In 2023, Hogan became the first woman commissioned as official Coronation artist. She has also worked with National Life Stories, an oral history charity organized through the British Library, to produce portraits of interview subjects as they record.

Throughout her career, Eileen Hogan's art has explored portraiture and landscape, especially greenery in urban spaces. She was appointed artist-in-residence at the Garden Museum in London in 2015, and painted a series of "London green spaces," nominated by her friends and colleagues. Her painting work is complemented by her artist's books, which reflect a strong interest in lettering, but Hogan has remained devoted to figurative painting throughout her career. She married her wife Cathy Courtney in 2015.
In addition to her current artistic practice, Hogan is a trustee at the Royal Drawing School and an ambassador for the Salvesen Mindroom Centre.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Eileen Hogan's concept for the Poetry Box came as the result of an invitation extended to her by the Victoria and Albert Museum. The museum asked her to respond to objects in their Japanese collection as part of the February to 31 March 2002 exhibition "Acts of renewal: Japanese art re-interpreted." An uta-garuta (歌ガルタ) set caught Hogan's attention.

Uta-garuta consists of two sets of 100 cards. The first set of 100 cards features the opening stanza of each of the 100 poems in the Ogura Hyakunin Ishu (百人一首). The second set features the concluding stanzas for each of the 100 poems in the Ogura Hyakunin Ishu. The goal is to match the opening and closing stanze of each poem. Hogan wanted to create an English language version of the game. Without a comparable poetic canon in English, she invited collaborators from a mix of nationalities and professions to submit a poem that held meaning for or had especially stuck with them.

The final result of the Poetry Box is a set of 80 cards. Half of the cards feature the first line of the poem on one side and a painting of the poet on the other. Half of the cards feature the last line of the poem on one side and a painting that interprets the poem itself on the other. Like in uta-garuta, the objective is to match the first and last line of the poem.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This series is arranged into the following subseries: Correspondence relating to the Poetry Box; Production of the Poetry Box; Recordings; Proofs; Poetry Box prototype. The first two subseries are restricted. Please contact the Yale Center for British Art Archives for further information.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series documents the steps taken to create the Poetry Box, including correspondence with individuals to select poems, grant requests, recordings with several selectors on why they chose the poem they did, and print proofs.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Material is arranged alphabetically by last name.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Each recording contains a discussion by a selector contacted for the Poetry Box and why they picked the poem they did.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Digitization is in process. For further information consult the Yale Center for British Art Archives.

did.unittitle: Original tape labels for Poetry Box recordings

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Material in the subseries contains proofs for individual potrait cards and for the booklets included with the Poetry Box.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Portraits have been placed first and arranged alphabetically, followed by page proofs.

did.unittitle: John Betjeman portrait card proof sheet
Edmund Blunden portrait dedicated to Elisabeth Fairman, oil painting

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Titled "Edmund Blunden for the Poetry Box" on verso; inscribed "for Elizabeth with much love, Eileen Hogan 2004 AD" on recto

did.unittitle: Thomas A. Clark representational image card proof sheet
W.H. Davies portrait card proof sheet
Lawrence Durrell representational image card proof sheet
Lawrence Ferlinghetti portrait card proof sheet
Portrait of Angus Lindsey representing image for Travis Venters poem card proof sheet
George Seferis portrait card proof sheet
Travis Venters portrait card proof sheet

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This series is arranged into four subseries: Paintings used as artwork for The Poetry Box, Paintings (portraits) not used as artwork for The Poetry Box, Paintings (representational images) not used as artwork for The Poetry Box, and Paintings (studies). Each subseries is arranged alphabetically by the poet's last name.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains the paintings for the cards used in the Poetry Box. It includes portraits of each poet and representations of their work done in watercolor. Also included are paintings that were eventully not used, as well as studies.

did.unittitle: Paintings used as artwork for The Poetry Box

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Material is arranged alphabetically by author surname.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Each folder contains two paintings: a portrait of the poem's author and a repersentation of the poem itself.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This subseries is arranged alphaebtically by author surname.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains studies for author portraits.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This subseries is arranged alphabetically by author surname.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains portraits of poets that were not used for the final version of the Poetry Box. A label states "19 paintings (portraits) not included in the Poetry Box mostly because of difficulties in finding accompanying images"

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This subseries is arranged alphabetically by surname of author.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains paintings representing poems, but were not used in the final version of the Poetry Box.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Material in this series relates to the exhibition of the Poetry Box as well as the original Victoria and Albert Museum exhibition that inspired the Poetry Box. It includes labels and booklets.

did.unittitle: "Acts of Renewal: Japanese Art Re-Interpreted," exhibition book
Victoria and Albert museum exhibition label for "Poetry Card Game"
Additional cards from the Poetry Box
The Poetry Box booklet and guide
The Poetry Box by Eileen Hogan exhibition book
